settings_language_desc: Interface language. Changing it reloads the app.
status_focused_app_hint: The daemon hasn't received any focus events yet. On KDE Plasma the focus bridge below must be loaded — use Repair if it isn't. On Sway / Hyprland it works out of the box via wlr-foreign-toplevel-management.
status_compat_known: ratbagd APIVersion={version} is within our supported range (expected {expected}). Should work; file a bug if you see anomalies.
status_compat_below_min: ratbagd APIVersion={version} is below the minimum gamerat has validated. gamerat may misbehave — consider upgrading libratbag.
status_compat_above_known: ratbagd APIVersion={version} is newer than the version gamerat was tested against ({expected}). This usually works, but the combination hasn't been explicitly validated — bugs may occur.
status_compat_unreachable_warning: ratbagd isn't running — gamerat-daemon can't apply profiles until it's started (systemctl start ratbagd).
status_softinput_hint: Software-input pipeline (uinput-backed toggles). Enable / disable from Settings; flips require a daemon restart.
status_softinput_popover_body: The daemon either can't open /dev/uinput or can't read your mouse's /dev/input/event* nodes — until that's fixed, pressing a soft-toggle button does nothing useful.
status_softinput_step_reboot: If a plain relogin doesn't pick the group up (KDE Plasma caches the gid set on its systemd --user manager), reboot — or run loginctl terminate-user $USER from a fresh TTY (Ctrl+Alt+F2).
status_softinput_step_status: Run gameratctl soft-input status for a per-piece breakdown.
status_bridge_hint: On KDE Plasma, gamerat observes window focus through the gamerat-focus KWin script. Auto-switching only works while it's loaded.
status_bridge_not_loaded_warning: The KWin focus script isn't loaded, so window auto-switching is inactive. Repair loads it now and enables it for future logins.
status_bridge_unknown_warning: Couldn't probe KWin. If auto-switching isn't working, try Repair.
btn_describe_disabled: Disabled — pressing this button has no effect.
rules_create_profile_first: Create a profile first — rules need something to reference.
rules_missing_title: No profile with this id exists
profiles_base_title: Edit the live hardware bindings on the reserved Desktop slot.
profiles_empty: No user profiles yet. Create one with the button above — DPI stages and button bindings are edited in the Mouse view once you select the profile here.
profiles_select_title: Select for editing — surfaces bindings + DPI in the Mouse view.
profiles_apply_title_auto: Autoswitch is on — profile selection is decided by rules. Turn off autoswitch in the header to apply manually.
profiles_apply_title_manual: Write this profile to the device now.
profiles_apply_base_title_auto: Autoswitch is on — base is applied automatically when no rule matches. Turn off autoswitch to apply manually.
profiles_apply_base_title_manual: Switch the device back to the reserved Desktop slot now.
games_rescan_title: Rescan: re-run the Steam / Lutris / Heroic scanners. Use this if a game is missing — e.g. its library drive mounted after the daemon started.
games_add_title: Add manually: register a game whose folder the scanners can't find by pasting its path.
games_no_profiles_hint: No profiles yet — the per-game profile picker is disabled until you create one in the Profiles panel.
games_none: No games discovered. (Are Steam / Lutris / Heroic installed?)
games_no_match: No games match the current filter.
games_dropdown_no_hint: No app_id_hint — add a rule manually in RULES.
games_dropdown_pick: Pick the profile to apply when this game gets focus. Choose 'base' to remove the rule and fall back to the desktop slot.
games_manual_intro: For games the Steam / Lutris / Heroic scanners can't find. The window match is what actually drives the profile — the path is informational.
games_manual_tip: Tip: focus the game, then check the FOCUS panel for the app_id it reports — that's the value to paste here.
devices_slots_empty: Daemon returned no slot info — the allocator hasn't seen this device yet.
devices_purge_body: This wipes every gamerat profile and rewrites all {count} slot(s) on {model} back to the canonical default profile. Useful before switching to another mouse tool (Piper, the libratbag CLI) so the device starts from a known clean state.
devices_purge_caveat: Rules are not wiped — only profiles + slot allocator state. Do not interrupt this operation: a kill mid-purge leaves the device half-defaulted, which self-heals on next daemon start but is messy.
mv_picker_title: Pick a saved profile to edit, or 'base' to see / write the active slot directly.
mv_base_hint: Editing the base layer — clicks write directly to the active hardware slot. Pick a profile above to edit a saved record instead.
mv_profile_hint: Editing profile {name}. Click any label to rebind that button — changes are {mode}.
mv_label_edit_hint: Click to edit binding for button {index}
mv_dpi_remove_disable: Remove stage — firmware will skip this slot in the DPI cycle.
mv_dpi_remove_nodisable: Remove stage from the profile. Note: this device doesn't support hardware-disabling stages, so the firmware will still cycle through the slot regardless.
mv_dpi_cap: Hardware caps DPI stages at {max} on this device.
mv_dpi_disable_hint: Removed stages are hardware-disabled — the firmware skips them when the DPI-cycle button is pressed.
mv_dpi_nodisable_hint: This device's driver doesn't advertise per-slot disable — the DPI-cycle button still walks every hardware slot regardless of the stages listed here.
mv_reset_known: Rewrite this profile with {device}'s factory bindings (from the per-device table in device-defaults.ts) and reset DPI to 800.
mv_reset_generic: No factory table for {device} — falls back to the generic Left/Right/Middle/Back/Forward on buttons 1–5, rest Disabled. Resets DPI to 800.
mv_save_apply_title: Save the profile and write it to the device now.
mv_timing_title: How long the last save/apply took, end to end. If this number spikes, that's worth reporting.
bind_key_fallback_summary: Or pick by name / enter a raw code
bind_macro_dsl_help: Comma-separated p:CODE (press) / r:CODE (release) / w:MS (wait) entries. Editing here overrides the recorder result.
bind_bad_macro_step: bad macro step "{entry}" (use p:30, r:30, w:25)
bind_toggle_disabled_flag: Enable "Software input augmentation" in Settings to use this.
bind_toggle_disabled_profile: Soft-macros require editing a managed profile (open one from the Profiles panel).
bind_warn_title: Macro leaves {keys} pressed when the button is released.
bind_warn_body: The OS will see those keys as held until you bind a release, run gameratctl panic, or unplug the mouse. Keep it if that's intentional (e.g. sticky-keys, hold-to-run), or auto-add a release for a clean momentary press.
bind_warn_suppress: Don't warn me again about unbalanced macros
bind_panic_btn_title: Force-release any stuck keys this macro left pressed, then auto-disable the binding.
bind_panic_countdown: Auto-disabling in {seconds}s. Cancel to keep the release-only macro for re-use.
bind_panic_cancelled: Auto-disable cancelled — release-only macro left in place.
bind_panic_superseded: Binding was changed in the meantime — left alone.
bind_panic_disabled: Binding disabled. Re-open to bind something new.
led_mode_unsupported: This device's LED {index} doesn't support {mode} mode
led_mono_hint: Monochrome LED — color is fixed by the firmware. Mode is the only adjustable axis.
macro_capturing: All keystrokes are being captured — click Stop when done.
macro_unknown_key: Unknown key {code} skipped — add to keycode-map.ts.
macro_no_steps: No steps yet. Click Record to start.
keycap_unknown: Unknown key {code} — not in the lookup table. Use the manual input below as a fallback.
